title: Self-Imposed Benching
description: You benched yourself briefly after a bad play. Then immediately un-benched yourself.

choices.text: Storm back in with maximum intensity
choices.flavor: The benching lasted eleven seconds. The comeback was immediate and aggressive.

choices.text: Take a breath, then calmly rejoin
choices.flavor: Brief timeout. Emotional reset. Back in the game. Maturity: unlocked.

title: The Faster Kid
description: You notice another kid is faster than you. This is the first time this has happened.

choices.text: Challenge them to race every single day
choices.flavor: Monday: lost. Tuesday: lost. Wednesday: lost by less. Progress.

choices.text: Switch to something you are better at
choices.flavor: You cannot be the fastest at everything. But you can be the best at throwing.
The wind was helping them. Your shoes were untied. Many external factors.

title: The Bigger Kid
description: A bigger kid at school keeps pushing you around at lunch. Today they took your dessert.

choices.flavor: You puff up your chest and say NO. They are surprised. It does not go perfectly, but you earned respect.
The teacher handles it. The bully gets in trouble. System works.
You avoid the conflict. Smart, but it stings a little.

title: Homework vs. Outside
description: Mom says homework first. The sun is out. The neighborhood kids are already playing.

choices.text: Do the homework first, play after
choices.flavor: You knock it out in 20 minutes. Mom is impressed. You still caught the last hour of daylight.

choices.text: Play first, homework in the dark
choices.flavor: The homework is sloppy. The handwriting is illegible. But you scored three touchdowns.
Half now, half later. Diplomacy wins. Both sides claim victory.

title: Talent Show Decision
description: The school talent show is coming up. Some kids are singing. Some are dancing.

choices.text: Do a sports trick routine on stage
choices.flavor: You juggle a ball on stage. The crowd goes wild. Future star energy.
You run the curtain and help other kids. The teacher calls you the MVP of the show.
Not your thing. Free time better spent running.
